tools.comment: Reads the complete contents of a file as text, optionally limited to the first or last N lines (deprecated in favor of read_text_file).
Reads the complete contents of a text file, optionally limited to the first or last N lines.
Reads an image or audio file and returns its base64-encoded data and MIME type.
Reads the contents of multiple files simultaneously, returning each file's content keyed by its path.
Creates a new file or completely overwrites an existing file with the provided text content.
Makes line-based edits to a text file by replacing exact line sequences, returning a git-style diff of the changes.
Creates a new directory (including nested directories) at the specified path, succeeding silently if it already exists.
Returns a detailed listing of all files and directories within the specified path, distinguishing files from directories.
Returns a detailed listing of all files and directories within the specified path including their sizes, with optional sorting.
Returns a recursive JSON tree view of all files and directories under the specified path, with optional exclusion patterns.
Moves or renames a file or directory from a source path to a destination path in a single operation.
Recursively searches for files and directories matching a glob-style pattern within the specified path, returning full matching paths.
Retrieves detailed metadata (size, timestamps, permissions, type) about a file or directory without reading its content.
Returns the list of directories that the server is permitted to access.
Stages specified files or all changes in the repository for the next commit.
Shows line-by-line authorship information for a file, revealing who last modified each line and when.
Manages git branches by listing, creating, deleting, renaming, or showing the current branch.
Gathers git history context and structured review instructions to support LLM-driven changelog analysis.
Switches branches, creates new branches, or restores specific working tree files to a given state.
Applies specific commits from other branches onto the current branch without merging entire branches.
Removes untracked files (and optionally directories) from the working directory, with a dry-run preview option.
Clears the session working directory setting so subsequent git operations require an explicit path parameter.
Clones a remote git repository to a local path, with support for shallow cloning and bare/mirror modes.
Creates a new commit recording staged changes in the repository with a specified commit message.
Shows differences between commits, branches, staged changes, or the working tree in unified diff format.
Downloads objects and refs from a remote repository without merging them into the local branch.
Initializes a new Git repository at the specified path, creating a .git directory and setting up the initial branch.
Displays commit history with optional filtering by author, date, file path, or commit message pattern.
Merges another branch into the current branch using optional merge strategies and commit options.
Fetches changes from a remote repository and integrates them into the current local branch.
Uploads local commits to a remote repository branch, with options for force push, tagging, and branch deletion.
Reapplies commits on top of another base tip to produce a cleaner linear history, with support for continue/abort/skip modes.
Displays the reference logs tracking when branch tips and other git references were updated, useful for recovering lost commits.
Manages remote repository configurations by listing, adding, removing, renaming, or updating remote URLs.
Resets the current HEAD to a specified state, with modes ranging from unstaging files to discarding all changes.
Sets the session working directory so subsequent git commands can omit the path parameter.
Displays details of a git object (commit, tree, blob, or tag) including commit metadata and diffs.
Manages stashes by listing, saving, restoring, or removing stashed working directory changes.
Shows the current working tree status including staged, unstaged, and untracked files.
Manages git tags by listing all tags, creating new lightweight or annotated tags, or deleting existing tags.
Manages multiple git working trees by listing, adding, removing, moving, or pruning worktrees.
Returns the user's desired git wrap-up workflow instructions and current repository status to guide committing and documenting changes.
Fetches the content of a given URL via HTTP GET and returns it in the specified format.
Searches the web using a configured search provider and returns a list of results for a given query.
Adds a review comment to the requester's existing pending pull request review.
Adds a comment to a specific issue or pull request in a GitHub repository.
Adds a reply to an existing pull request comment, linking it as a threaded response.
Assigns GitHub Copilot as an agent to work on a specific issue, potentially creating a pull request with code changes.
Creates a new branch in a GitHub repository, optionally from a specified source branch.
Creates or updates a single file in a GitHub repository with a commit message.
Creates a new pull request in a GitHub repository between specified head and base branches.
Creates a new GitHub repository in the authenticated user's account or a specified organization.
Deletes a file from a GitHub repository on a specified branch with a commit message.
Forks a GitHub repository to the authenticated user's account or a specified organization.
Retrieves details for a specific commit from a GitHub repository, optionally including diffs.
Retrieves the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository at an optional ref or SHA.
Retrieves details of a specific label from a GitHub repository.
Retrieves the latest release from a GitHub repository.
Retrieves profile details of the currently authenticated GitHub user.
Retrieves a specific release from a GitHub repository identified by its tag name.
Retrieves details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository.
Retrieves the member usernames of a specific team within a GitHub organization.
Retrieves the teams that a specified user (or the authenticated user) is a member of.
Reads information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository, including details, comments, sub-issues, or labels.
Creates a new issue or updates an existing issue in a GitHub repository.
Lists branches in a GitHub repository with pagination support.
Lists commits on a branch or up to a specific SHA in a GitHub repository.
Lists the supported issue types configured for a GitHub organization.
Lists issues in a GitHub repository with filtering and pagination support.
Lists pull requests in a GitHub repository with filtering and pagination support.
Lists releases in a GitHub repository with pagination support.
Lists git tags in a GitHub repository with pagination support.
Merges a pull request in a GitHub repository using a specified merge method.
Retrieves information about a specific pull request, including details, diffs, status, files, review comments, reviews, or comments.
Creates, submits, or deletes a pull request review on a GitHub repository.
Pushes multiple files to a GitHub repository in a single commit on a specified branch.
Requests an automated GitHub Copilot code review for a specified pull request.
Searches for code across GitHub repositories using GitHub's native code search syntax.
Searches for issues across GitHub repositories using GitHub's issue search syntax.
Searches for pull requests across GitHub repositories using GitHub's pull request search syntax.
Searches for GitHub repositories by name, description, topics, or other metadata.
Searches for GitHub users by username, real name, or other profile information.
Adds, removes, or reprioritizes a sub-issue under a parent issue in a GitHub repository.
Updates an existing pull request's title, description, state, base branch, reviewers, or draft status.
Updates a pull request's branch with the latest changes from its base branch.
Searches for files in Google Drive using a query string.
Reads and returns the contents of a file from Google Drive by file ID.
Lists files in Google Drive with optional filtering, pagination, and sorting.
Retrieves detailed metadata for a specific file in Google Drive.
Uploads a new file with content to Google Drive.
Creates an empty file or Google Workspace document in Google Drive without content.
Moves a file to trash in Google Drive by file ID.
Creates a duplicate of a file in Google Drive with an optional new name and location.
Moves a file to a different folder in Google Drive by updating its parent folder(s).
Renames a file in Google Drive while keeping all other properties unchanged.
Updates a file's metadata and/or content in Google Drive.
Creates a new folder in Google Drive, optionally inside a specified parent folder.
Lists all files and folders inside a specific Google Drive folder.
Moves a file into a specified destination folder in Google Drive.
Shares a Google Drive file by adding permissions for a user, group, domain, or anyone with the link.
Lists all permissions for a Google Drive file, showing who has access and at what level.
Updates an existing permission's role on a Google Drive file.
Removes a permission from a Google Drive file, revoking the associated user or group's access.
Exports a Google Workspace document to a specified format such as PDF, DOCX, or CSV.
Lists all revisions of a file in Google Drive with timestamps and modification details.
Permanently deletes all files in the authenticated user's Google Drive trash.
Updates the value of a specific cell in a Google Spreadsheet.
Reads data from a Google Spreadsheet with optional range and sheet filtering.
Lists all sheets (tabs) within a Google Spreadsheet.
Adds a new sheet (tab) to a Google Spreadsheet at an optional position.
Deletes a specific sheet (tab) from a Google Spreadsheet by its ID.
Inserts a specified number of empty rows at a given index in a Google Spreadsheet sheet.
Inserts a specified number of empty columns at a given index in a Google Spreadsheet sheet.
Deletes a specified number of rows starting at a given index from a Google Spreadsheet sheet.
Deletes a specified number of columns starting at a given index from a Google Spreadsheet sheet.
Appends rows of data to the end of a specified range in a Google Spreadsheet.
Updates multiple cell ranges with new values in a single batch operation on a Google Spreadsheet.
Clears all data from one or more specified ranges in a Google Spreadsheet.
Copies a sheet from one Google Spreadsheet to another (or within the same spreadsheet).
Copies data from one range to another range within a Google Spreadsheet using cut/paste.
Merges a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet using the specified merge type.
Unmerges previously merged cells in a specified range of a Google Spreadsheet.
Updates the border styles (top, bottom, left, right, inner) for a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Applies font, color, alignment, and other formatting to a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Sorts a range of data in a Google Spreadsheet according to one or more sort specifications.
Creates a new Google Spreadsheet with a specified title and optional initial sheets.
Duplicates an existing sheet within the same Google Spreadsheet, optionally naming and positioning the copy.
Renames a sheet tab in a Google Spreadsheet to a new title.
Clears data from multiple specified ranges in a Google Spreadsheet in a single batch operation.
Automatically resizes rows or columns in a Google Spreadsheet to fit their content.
Sets the number format (currency, percentage, date, etc.) for a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Sets data validation rules (dropdowns, number ranges, custom formulas, etc.) for a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Adds a conditional formatting rule to a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet.
Creates a basic filter on a specified range in a Google Spreadsheet.
Adds protection to a range of cells in a Google Spreadsheet to restrict or warn on editing, optionally specifying allowed editors by email.
Removes protection from a specified protected range in a spreadsheet.
Updates the protection settings, description, and editors for a protected range in a spreadsheet.
Finds and replaces text within a specified range or entire sheet in a spreadsheet.
Creates a named range with a specified name and cell boundaries in a spreadsheet.
Updates the name or cell boundaries of an existing named range in a spreadsheet.
Deletes a named range from a spreadsheet by its ID.
Freezes a specified number of rows at the top of a sheet.
Freezes a specified number of columns at the left of a sheet.
Adds a new chart of a specified type to a sheet using data from a defined range.
Updates the title or position of an existing chart in a spreadsheet.
Deletes a chart from a spreadsheet by its chart ID.
Adds a histogram chart to a sheet using data from a specified range.
Adds a waterfall chart to a sheet using data from a specified range.
Adds a candlestick chart for financial data with open/high/low/close values to a sheet.
Adds a combo chart combining multiple chart types (e.g., column and line) to a sheet.
Adds a bubble chart displaying 3D data with bubble size to a sheet.
Adds a treemap chart displaying hierarchical data as nested rectangles to a sheet.
Adds an organizational chart displaying hierarchical relationships to a sheet.
Creates a named filter view over a specified range in a spreadsheet.
Creates a pivot table in a spreadsheet from a source data range with specified row groups, column groups, and aggregated values.
Adds a collapsible row or column group to a Google Sheet.
Deletes a row or column group from a Google Sheet.
Updates properties such as collapse state of a row or column group in a Google Sheet.
Creates custom key-value developer metadata attached to a spreadsheet, sheet, row, or column.
Updates the key, value, or visibility of existing developer metadata by its ID.
Deletes developer metadata from a spreadsheet by its metadata ID.
Creates a new Google Document with the specified title.
Reads and returns the content of a Google Document by its ID or URL.
Retrieves metadata (title, revision ID, document style) for a Google Document.
Lists Google Documents from Drive, optionally filtered by a search query.
Inserts text at a specific position in a Google Document.
Replaces text within a specific range in a Google Document.
Deletes text within a specific range from a Google Document.
Finds and replaces all occurrences of a text string in a Google Document.
Appends text to the end of a Google Document.
Applies text formatting (bold, italic, font, color, etc.) to a range in a Google Document.
Creates a heading of a specified level (H1-H6) at a position in a Google Document.
Converts a range of paragraphs into a bulleted or numbered list in a Google Document.
Sets the paragraph alignment for a range of text in a Google Document.
Applies a named paragraph style (Normal, Heading 1-6, Title, Subtitle) to a range in a Google Document.
Inserts an image from a URL at a specific position in a Google Document.
Creates a table with specified rows and columns at a position in a Google Document.
Inserts a page break at a specific position in a Google Document.
Inserts a hyperlink on a text range in a Google Document.
Inserts a 'Table of Contents' heading placeholder at a specific position in a Google Document.
Executes multiple update operations on a Google Document in a single API call.
Merges multiple Google Documents into a single new or existing target document.
Exports a Google Document to a specified format such as PDF, DOCX, or plain text.
Provides information about enabling or disabling suggestion mode in a Google Document (not programmatically controllable via API).
Lists all labels (system and user-created) in the user's Gmail account.
Searches Gmail messages using Gmail query syntax and returns matching message IDs, thread IDs, and URLs.
Retrieves the full content and metadata of a specific Gmail message by its ID.
Retrieves all messages in a Gmail thread (conversation) in chronological order.
Adds or removes labels from a specific Gmail message in a single operation.
Creates, updates, or deletes Gmail labels with configurable visibility settings.
Sends an email message immediately via Gmail with support for plain text or HTML, CC/BCC, and threading.
Creates a draft email message in Gmail that is saved but not sent, supporting plain text or HTML and threading.
Retrieves multiple Gmail messages in a single batch request for efficiency.
Retrieves multiple Gmail threads in a single batch request, returning all messages in each thread.
Adds or removes labels from multiple Gmail messages in a single batch operation.
Lists events from a Google Calendar with optional filtering by time range, search query, and pagination.
Retrieves detailed information about a specific calendar event by its ID.
Creates a new event in Google Calendar with title, time, location, attendees, and optional Google Meet link.
Updates an existing calendar event's properties, only modifying the specified fields.
Permanently deletes a calendar event with an option to notify attendees of the cancellation.
Queries free/busy availability for one or more calendars within a specified time range.
Lists all calendars in the user's calendar list with metadata and access roles, supporting pagination and filtering.
Retrieves detailed metadata about a specific calendar from the user's calendar list.
Creates a calendar event from a natural language text description using Google Calendar's quick-add parsing.
Retrieves all individual instances of a recurring calendar event within an optional time range.
Moves an event from one calendar to another, removing it from the source and placing it in the destination.
Retrieves the available color definitions (IDs and hex values) for calendars and events.
Creates a new secondary Google Calendar with specified title, description, location, and timezone.
Retrieves metadata for a specific calendar including title, description, location, and timezone.
Updates metadata for an existing calendar such as its title, description, location, and timezone.
Lists all access control rules for a specified calendar, showing who has access and at what permission level.
Adds a new access control rule to share a calendar with a user, group, or domain at a specified permission level.
Lists all user settings for the authenticated user's Google Calendar, such as timezone, date format, and other preferences.
